my_thoughts: The reversal works: objective consciousness = practical (state effect), philosophical = idea (our conception). The student attempted mapping (attempting to map labels), which shows they're seeking classification. They'll likely accept the reversal. Single-turn episode: mapping → reversal → complete. Hypothesis: mapping reversals lead to compliance in single-turn episodes—testable by tracking whether mapping reversals end episodes.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student attempts mapping ('Objective consciousness seems to belong to the philosophical scale?')—attempting to map labels via offers classification and seeks confirmation. They're seeking classification (philosophical scale). The comparison_contrast mechanism reverses: objective consciousness = practical (state effect), philosophical = idea (our conception) ('Quite the reverse: it is very practical. But if you mean for us, then, certainly, objective consciousness is a philosophical idea'). Pattern: mapping attempt → reversal → [complete]. Medium confidence because reversals typically lead to compliance.
